entries.title: HOMILY 18.3 on deuteronomy 1:31
entries.excerpt: See what we are generally taught about God: "God is not as a man to be deceived nor as the son of man to be threatened," and we learn that God is not as man. But other texts say that God is as a man: "For the Lord your God has taught you as a man teaches his son," and again, "As a man he takes on the manners of his son." Hence, wherever the Scriptures speak theologically about God in relation to himself and do not involve his plan for human matters, they teach that he is "not as a man." For "there will be no limit to his greatness," and "he is more feared than all of the gods," and "praise him, all you angels of God; praise him, all his hosts; praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all stars and light." You can find many other passages in the sacred Scriptures to which you can relate the words "God is not as a man."
